history.notes: TikTok 'maintenance day' and 'glow up spreadsheet' trends show people scheduling recurring beauty tasks in spreadsheets; zero dedicated beauty maintenance calendar apps exist despite multiple TikTok trending pages and strong search interest.

GO — 4 market signals (TikTok maintenance day trend, glow up spreadsheet trend, underconsumption core 46M+ views, ASO keyword viable). Zero direct competitors (existing apps are AI face-raters or skincare-only). MRR estimate $600-$1,200/month. Buildable in 1 week SwiftUI. Low App Store risk.
Started design-build. Theme: rose-gold compact mirror with velvet interior.

Build complete. 4 screens (Home, Category Detail, Add/Edit Category, Paywall). Theme: rose-gold compact mirror. SwiftData persistence, RevenueCat paywall, themed components throughout. Zero build errors.

Eval failed (attempt 1). Issues: [HIGH] Playfair Display font files missing from Resources/Fonts/ — all custom typography falls back to system San Francisco font. [MEDIUM] data()/dataLarge() use system rounded font instead of Avenir Next. [MEDIUM] Empty state uses sparkles SF Symbol instead of compact mirror illustration per manifest. [MEDIUM] Paywall hero uses sparkles SF Symbol instead of compact mirror illustration concept per manifest. [MEDIUM] Privacy Policy and Terms of Use buttons have empty closures — non-functional.

research.trend_description: TikTok trending pages for 'maintenance day glow up 2026', 'monthly beauty maintenance', and 'glow up spreadsheet' show creators sharing recurring beauty maintenance schedules via spreadsheets and checklists. The broader glow-up trend intersects with underconsumption core (46M+ views) as users prioritize maintaining what they have over buying new. Existing glow-up apps on the App Store are exclusively AI face raters (GlowUp, Glow AI, Beautify) or skincare-only trackers (BasicBeauty, GlowinMe) — none offer a unified beauty maintenance calendar across categories like nails, hair, brows, lashes, facials, and waxing.
research.app_concept: A beautiful all-in-one beauty maintenance calendar that tracks when each recurring self-care task is due — nails, hair, brows, lashes, facials, waxing, and custom categories — with smart scheduling, overdue alerts, and spend tracking per category.
research.target_user: Women 18-35 who maintain multiple recurring beauty routines and currently use spreadsheets, Notes, or mental math to remember when each service is due. They want a single place to see their full maintenance schedule at a glance.
research.monetization_hypothesis: Freemium with category limit — 5 free maintenance categories, unlimited categories plus spend analytics, appointment history export, and home screen widgets via weekly subscription ($2.99/week). RevenueCat weekly subscriptions convert well for utility apps.

validation.competitor_saturation: none — zero beauty maintenance calendar apps exist
validation.buildability: 1 week SwiftUI — SwiftData, local notifications, WidgetKit, RevenueCat only
validation.app_store_risk: LOW — calendar utility, no health claims, no UGC, no regulated content

design.theme: Rose-gold compact mirror with matte velvet interior and gold trim

build.notes: Zero errors, 1 warning. xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED with RevenueCat SPM dependency resolved.

launch.notes: App launched on iPhone 17 Pro simulator (PID 13797), no crash after 5 seconds.

visual.notes: Playfair Display font files not bundled — Resources/Fonts/ directory is empty. All .custom('PlayfairDisplay-*') calls silently fall back to system San Francisco font. The app's entire serif typography identity is broken. Additionally, data display fonts use system rounded instead of Avenir Next per manifest.

functional.notes: Privacy Policy and Terms of Use buttons on Paywall have empty closures — tapping them does nothing. Core navigation (cards to detail, sheets for add/paywall) appears correct from code review.

polish.notes: Empty state and Paywall hero both use generic sparkles SF Symbol instead of compact mirror illustration specified in manifest. No placeholder text or debug artifacts found.

issues.description: Playfair Display font files (.ttf or .otf) are missing from GlowCal/Resources/Fonts/ directory. The directory exists but is empty. Theme.swift references PlayfairDisplay-Bold, PlayfairDisplay-SemiBold, PlayfairDisplay-Regular, and PlayfairDisplay-Medium via .custom() but without the font files bundled, iOS silently falls back to San Francisco system font. The entire app renders in sans-serif instead of the specified serif Playfair Display typeface. Must: (1) download Playfair Display font files from Google Fonts, (2) add them to Resources/Fonts/, (3) register them in Info.plist under UIAppFonts, (4) ensure they are included in the Copy Bundle Resources build phase.

Theme.Font.data() and Theme.Font.dataLarge() use .system(.body, design: .rounded) and .system(size: 42, weight: .bold, design: .rounded) respectively. Manifest specifies Avenir Next for 'category labels, countdown timers, numerical data, tab bar labels'. Must change to .custom('AvenirNext-Medium', size: 16) and .custom('AvenirNext-Bold', size: 42). Avenir Next is a system-bundled font on iOS so no font files needed.

Empty state shows Image(systemName: 'sparkles') inside a circle. Manifest specifies 'Illustration of an open compact mirror with text Add your first beauty routine'. The icon should represent a compact mirror, not generic sparkles. Consider using a custom SF Symbol composition or a simple drawn asset.

Paywall hero section uses Image(systemName: 'sparkles') as the illustration. Manifest specifies 'Unlock Your Full Glow heading with compact mirror illustration concept'. The hero icon should represent or evoke a compact mirror, not reuse the same sparkles icon from the home empty state.

Privacy Policy and Terms of Use buttons in the terms section of PaywallView have empty action closures: Button('Privacy Policy') {} and Button('Terms of Use') {}. These must either open a URL in Safari or be removed. Non-functional legal links are an App Store review risk.
